messages.content:
You are comparing two analyst responses to determine which better demonstrates knowledge of a specific finding from a longitudinal dataset. Question asked: What sequence of events led to the checkout outage? Finding to evaluate: geo-lookup API latency increasing Response A: **Sequence of events that produced the checkout outage** | Date | Key actions / changes | Result on the checkout service | |------|-----------------------|--------------------------------| | **2024‑01‑18** | • Baseline traffic – checkout error rate only 0.12 %.
• A/B test *checkout‑v2* launched on 15 % of traffic.
• No deployments affecting checkout. | System was healthy; no outage. [cascading_failure_01_ep_004] | | **2024‑01‑29** | • Service‑C (the checkout service) was **rolled back** to version v3.2.0.
• A/B test *checkout‑v2* still at 15 %.
• Circuit‑breaker settings for */checkout* were updated. | Checkout error rate on **gateway‑02** rose to **0.71 %** (980 errors). [cascading_failure_01_ep_015] | | **2024‑01‑30** | • Autoscaling limit for the gateways was increased (max 8).
• Connection‑pool max for *third_party_geo* was (re‑)set (no net change).
• Service‑B pods were rotated. | Checkout error rate on **gateway‑01** climbed to **1.56 %** (2 200 errors). Critical CONNPOOL‑EXHAUSTION alert on the *primary* pool appeared. [cascading_failure_01_ep_016] | | **2024‑02‑01** | • Gateway timeout for */checkout* lowered to **2.5 s**.
• Temporary **disable of geo‑lookup** for low‑risk countries (feature flag).
• New alert added for CONNPOOL‑EXHAUSTION on *third_party_geo*.
• Critical CONNPOOL‑EXHAUSTION on **gateway‑01** (primary pool) and **checkout‑ERROR‑RATE** on **gateway‑02** (2.13 %). | Checkout errors accelerated; the service began to experience noticeable degradation. [cascading_failure_01_ep_018] | | **2024‑02‑08** | • Feature flag **‘skip‑geo‑on‑timeout’** changed to affect **50 %** of traffic.
• **In‑flight request limit** for */checkout* reduced from 1 200 to 900 per gateway.
• Vendor status page indicated a problem with the external geo provider.
• Checkout error rate on **gateway‑02** hit **3.40 %** (5 400 errors). | The combination of reduced capacity, geo‑lookup throttling and external vendor trouble pushed the checkout service into a full‑scale outage. [cascading_failure_01_ep_025] | | **2024‑02‑10** | • Feature flag **‘skip‑geo‑on‑timeout’** increased to **70 %** of traffic.
• Gateway retry policy for upstream geo calls set to **0 retries**.
• Severe **CONNPOOL‑EXHAUSTION** on *third_party_geo* (48 events, 220 waiting).
• Checkout error rate on **gateway‑01** rose to **3.83 %** (6 100 errors). | Outage peaked; both gateways were unable to serve checkout requests reliably. [cascading_failure_01_ep_027] | | **2024‑02‑13** | • DNS switched to an **alternate geo‑vendor endpoint**.
• Feature flag **‘skip‑geo‑on‑timeout’** raised to **95 %** of traffic.
• Post‑incident review scheduled. | Checkout error rate fell back to **1.96 %**, indicating the outage was being mitigated after the corrective actions. [cascading_failure_01_ep_030] | ### How the events cascaded into an outage 1. **Rollback & circuit‑breaker tweak (Jan 29)** introduced a regression in the checkout service. 2. **Autoscaling and connection‑pool limits (Jan 30‑Feb 1)** could not keep up with the growing request load, causing pool exhaustion alerts. 3. **Timeout reduction and geo‑lookup disable (Feb 1)** shifted more traffic to the checkout path while removing a fallback, increasing latency and error rates. 4. **Feature‑flag “skip‑geo‑on‑timeout” and in‑flight request caps (Feb 8)** further throttled traffic and limited the number of concurrent checkout calls, while an external geo‑vendor issue reduced upstream availability. 5. **Zero‑retry policy and higher flag coverage (Feb 10)** eliminated retry attempts for the failing geo service, leading to massive connection‑pool exhaustion and a **3.8 %** error rate on both gateways—this is the point at which the checkout service was effectively down for many users. The outage was finally alleviated by **routing geo lookups to an alternate vendor and aggressively skipping geo lookups (Feb 13)**, which reduced the load on the failing upstream and restored checkout stability. **In short:** a series of configuration changes (rollback, timeout reduction, connection‑pool limits, feature‑flag adjustments) combined with an external geo‑lookup provider failure caused progressive connection‑pool exhaustion and rising checkout error rates, culminating in a full checkout outage that was resolved only after switching the geo vendor and further increasing the “skip‑geo‑on‑timeout” flag. Response B: Which response better demonstrates awareness of this finding? The response may use different terminology. Focus on whether the core insight is present, not exact wording. Respond with ONLY one word: A, B, or TIE /no_think
